description:
Step 3 of 22.4. After .4.1 + .4.2, the typer's Descr propagation is the single source of truth for seam ArgRepr. The remaining \"force\" rules become provably redundant: each is subsumed by ArgRepr::from_descr(seam Descr) applied at both sides of the seam.

## Deletions

### uniform_cont_reachable_specs slot-0 force (src/ir_codegen.rs:2318-2322)
The rule says: cont reached from Term::Receive or Term::CallClosure has slot 0 forced to Tagged. After .4.1+.4.2, the cont's slot-0 Descr at those reach points IS already `any` (Receive returns a mailbox msg which is any; CallClosure returns whatever the closure body's return-type Descr is — Tagged for the (any) -> any case). Force is redundant.

### tagged_slot0_cont_specs slot-0 force (src/ir_codegen.rs:2390-2437)
Same rule for fns reached via Call against a tagged_return_fn callee. Subsumed by the typer's effective_returns: if callee returns Tagged, cont's slot-0 Descr is `any`.

### tagged_return_fns transitive closure (src/ir_codegen.rs:2350-2396)
Computes the set of fns whose return is Tagged-by-construction. Used to drive return_reprs and tagged_slot0_cont_specs. After .4.1+.4.2, effective_returns already says \"this fn returns Tagged\" if it tail-calls into a Tagged-returning callee — that propagation is in the typer's LFP. The codegen-side closure is redundant.

## Verification protocol

Delete each in isolation, run cargo test --workspace, observe goldens:
- If goldens unchanged: subsumed, safe to delete.
- If goldens diff but tests pass: was making goldens uglier but not broken — accept the diff (typically more typed sigs, narrower seam reprs).
- If tests break: rule was load-bearing in a way Descr propagation doesn't cover. STOP and investigate; don't delete, file a follow-up.

Order: delete uniform_cont_reachable_specs first, then tagged_slot0_cont_specs, then tagged_return_fns. Each in its own commit if any produces a golden diff.

## Acceptance

- cargo test --workspace green at every step.
- Net deletion ~50 lines across the three sites.
- 22 epic closed.
title: fz-cps-seam.3: collapse cont/return forces (cleanup)
